<!-- Thanks for contributing! The 60-second version: README.md, projects.yaml,
TAGS.md, harnesses.json, and llms.txt are GENERATED by scripts/generate.py —
PRs that edit them directly can't merge. Data changes go in generate.py
(PROJECTS + META + AXES); then run `python3 scripts/generate.py` and commit
the regenerated outputs too. Prefer zero setup? Open an issue instead —
accepted suggestions get ported for you, with co-author credit either way. -->

**What kind of change is this?**

- [ ] Add a project → fill in the intake below
- [ ] Correct a project I maintain (description / example link / tier / axes score)
- [ ] Scripts, site, docs, or CI
- [ ] Other:

**Description:**



---

## Project intake (required when adding a project)

**The project**

- Repo URL:
- Proposed category (one of the README's ten):
- One sentence on what the **harness** is — the agent loop, tool wiring, approval model, or recovery layer, as distinct from the UI shell:
- Closest existing entry on the list, and what this adds over it:
- Best "show me it in action" link (a docs page, example dir, or leaderboard — not the repo root):

**Disclosure**

- [ ] I maintain or am affiliated with this project *(totally fine — most good submissions are; just say so)*
- [ ] I'm an unaffiliated user of it

**Quality self-check** — this is how the repo gets validated, so save a round-trip and check it yourself:

- [ ] It owns or structures an agent loop — not a model wrapper, prompt pack, or demo app
- [ ] A `LICENSE` file exists — name it here: ______ *(source-available is fine, it's flagged ⚠️; none/unclear renders ❓)*
- [ ] Active: a commit within the last 60 days, and the repo is not archived
- [ ] Real usage signals: roughly 100+ stars and >10 commits — or honest context on why it belongs anyway
- [ ] The README lets a stranger run it: install steps plus at least one working example
- [ ] Not already on the list, the [Graveyard](https://github.com/RyanAlberts/best-of-Agent-Harnesses#%EF%B8%8F-graveyard), or [the radar](https://github.com/RyanAlberts/best-of-Agent-Harnesses#-on-the-radar) — search the README first

Below the bar today? Say so directly — promising projects get pinned to 🔭 On the radar instead of declined, and graduate as they grow.

**Mechanics (only if your PR edits code or data)**

- [ ] Changes are in `scripts/generate.py` (PROJECTS + META + AXES), not in generated files
- [ ] I ran `python3 scripts/generate.py` and committed the regenerated outputs
- [ ] `python3 -m pytest tests/ -q` passes
